questions.question: What is discussed in Paragraph 2?

options.A: The great pleasure that sports give to fans.
options.B: The different roles that athletes play in life.
options.C: The change that sports bring to the whole world.
options.D: The qualities that athletes show during competition.

questions.passage:
These days, it seems, the sports pages have come to read like the police news. The fan looking for game scores must first read news stories about drug use among athletes and reports of other serious crimes. "What's going on here?" sports fans ask. What's happening to our heroes?

It is not difficult to understand our desire for athletes to be heroes. On the surface, at least, athletes show many of the classical qualities of heroes. And sports allow us to see acts that can truly be described as courageous, exciting, beautiful, and even noble. In an ever-changing world, sports is still an area in which we can regularly experience a certain kind of greatness.

Yet there's something strange here. The qualities a society looks for in its heroes—selflessness and social awareness—are exactly the opposite of those needed to become a successful athlete. Becoming a star athlete requires extreme self-absorption, extraordinary physical skills, and a very competitive personality. These qualities may make a great athlete, but they don't necessarily make a great person. Moreover, our society strengthens these qualities with the system it has created to produce athletes—a system that gives athletes the sense they are stars who can do whatever they want.

Young athletes learn that success, rather than hard work and honest play, is what brings rewards. And for those successful enough to rise to big-time college sports, the "reward" is often an artificially controlled social environment that frees them from many of the responsibilities other students face. Coaches—whose own jobs depend on maintaining winning sports programs—watch over their athletes to make sure that nothing affects their ability to compete. If an athlete gets into trouble with the police, for instance, the coach will probably try to take care of things. In some schools, athletes don't even choose their own classes or buy their own books; the coach does it all.

Given this situation, it's not too surprising that many young American athletes seem to think of themselves as special or even privileged (有特权的) people, and it grows worse the longer they participate in athletics.

Needless to say, not all athletes are like this. There are plenty of athletes who want to help others. After retiring from football, Alan Page became a successful lawyer and built the Page Education Foundation, which helps young people around the country pay for college. Thankfully, there will always be some true heroes to be found among professional athletes.

Still, it's probably ________ for society to look to athletes for its heroes—any more than to look to actors or lawyers. The social role played by athletes is indeed important, but it's different from that of heroes.
questions.passage_source: 2018年7月 北京西城期末 · 阅读D (Q41-Q44)

questions.question: 语法填空第1题：George ________ (go) out half an hour ago.
questions.passage:
语法填空（独立句）

1. George ________ (go) out half an hour ago. Can I have him call you back?
2. The talk was given by a man ________ has a background in zoology.
3. ________ the car is old, it still runs well.
4. It is reported that a space station ________ (build) on the moon in years to come.
5. ________ (keep) your bike in good condition, follow this simple advice.
6. The weather turned out to be very good, ________ was more than we expected.
7. It's a very select club. I ________ (try) to join it for years.
8. At first I was very nervous, but ________ a while I began to feel more confident.
9. Some pre-school children go to a daycare center, ________ they learn simple games and songs.
10. I really appreciate ________ (have) time to relax with you on this nice island.

questions.question: What does the woman recommend?

options.C: A restaurant with good food for special occasions

questions.explanation: 参见听力原文第 1 题相关部分。
questions.passage:
Text 1
M: Hi, I'm looking for a nice restaurant for a birthday dinner. It doesn't have to be fancy, but the food should be really good.
W: Well, my recommendation would be La Maison. It's not particularly expensive, but it has outstanding cuisine. People love it for special occasions.

Text 2
W: I was just given a ticket to the art exhibition next week. Would you like to come with me? It's the one featuring local artists.
M: Oh, that sounds great! But next week is pretty busy for me. I have a work conference from Monday to Thursday. Could we go on Friday?
W: Sure, that would be perfect.

Text 3
W: Hello, I'd like to return this jacket. I bought it here two weeks ago.
M: Is there anything wrong with it?
W: Well, the zipper is broken.
M: I'm sorry to hear that. Do you have your receipt?
W: Yes, I do.
M: Good. We can exchange it for a new one or give you a full refund.
W: I'd prefer a new one, please.

Text 4
W: How did your presentation go this morning?
M: Not as well as I hoped. I got really nervous and lost my train of thought halfway through.
W: Oh, no. But from what I've heard, the content was impressive. People seemed interested.
M: Yes, the questions I got afterward were very engaging. It's just the initial delivery I need to work on.

Text 5
M: Wow, your garden looks amazing! All those vegetables and flowers – how do you find the time?
W: Well, I gave up watching so much TV last spring. I realised I had a lot more free time once I stopped doing that.

Text 6
W: Excuse me. I'm looking for the conference room. I'm here for the marketing seminar.
M: Sure, it's on the fourth floor. Take the elevator to the right. When you get out, turn left, and it's the third door down the hall.
W: Thank you. And can you tell me where the restrooms are?
M: They're on this floor, just past the main entrance to your left.
W: Great. I appreciate your help.
M: No problem. Enjoy the seminar.

Text 7
M: I was thinking about going to the mountains this weekend. Want to come?
W: I'd love to, but the weather forecast says there might be heavy rain. Wouldn't it be better to go to the beach instead?
M: Actually, I checked the weather this morning. They updated it – now they're saying partly cloudy with temperatures in the mid-twenties. The mountains should be perfect.
W: Oh, that's great news! In that case, let's definitely go to the mountains. I love hiking there.
M: Wonderful! I'll book us a cabin for Saturday night.

Text 8
W: Did you read the article about the new subway line? They're going to extend it all the way to the airport.
M: Yes, I saw that. It's about time! The traffic on the highway to the airport is terrible.
W: Exactly! And the taxi fees are so expensive. The subway will be so much more convenient.
M: When do they expect it to be finished?
W: According to the article, construction will start next spring and the line should open in about two years.
M: That's exciting news for commuters.

Text 9
M: Good afternoon. I'm here to pick up my car. I dropped it off this morning for a service.
W: Of course. Can I have your name?
M: It's Robert Taylor.
W: Let me check... Yes, Mr. Taylor. The standard oil change and filter replacement have been done.
M: Great. Was everything else okay with the car?
W: Our mechanic noticed that your front tires are getting a little worn. It's not urgent, but you might want to think about replacing them soon.
M: Thanks for the heads-up. I'll think about it. So how much do I owe?
W: That'll be one hundred and twenty dollars for today's service.

Text 10
M: Have you ever thought about volunteering abroad?
W: I've always wanted to, but I'm not sure where to start. Do you have any experience with it?
M: Actually yes. Last summer, I spent two months teaching English to children in Thailand. It was an incredible experience.
W: That sounds amazing! Was it through an organization?
M: Yes, I went through a nonprofit called Global Volunteers. They help you with all the logistics – accommodation, meals, even language training.
W: That's very helpful. I'm particularly interested in environmental work. Do they have programs for that?
M: They do. They have a marine conservation program in Costa Rica and a reforestation project in Kenya.
W: The Kenya project sounds perfect. I've always wanted to visit Africa, and I care deeply about the environment.
M: I think you'd love it. Just make sure to apply well in advance – spots fill up quickly.
questions.passage_source: 2026年1月 浙江卷 · 听力理解 (Q1-Q20)

M: Not as well as I hoped. I got really nervous and lost my train of thought halfway through.
W: Oh, no. But from what I've heard, the content was impressive. People seemed interested.
M: Yes, the questions I got afterward were very engaging. It's just the initial delivery I need to work on.

Text 5
M: Wow, your garden looks amazing! All those vegetables and flowers – how do you find the time?
W: Well, I gave up watching so much TV last spring. I realised I had a lot more free time once I stopped doing that.

Text 6
W: Excuse me. I'm looking for the conference room. I'm here for the marketing seminar.
M: Sure, it's on the fourth floor. Take the elevator to the right. When you get out, turn left, and it's the third door down the hall.
W: Thank you. And can you tell me where the restrooms are?
M: They're on this floor, just past the main entrance to your left.
W: Great. I appreciate your help.
M: No problem. Enjoy the seminar.

Text 7
M: I was thinking about going to the mountains this weekend. Want to come?
W: I'd love to, but the weather forecast says there might be heavy rain. Wouldn't it be better to go to the beach instead?
M: Actually, I checked the weather this morning. They updated it – now they're saying partly cloudy with temperatures in the mid-twenties. The mountains should be perfect.
W: Oh, that's great news! In that case, let's definitely go to the mountains. I love hiking there.
M: Wonderful! I'll book us a cabin for Saturday night.

Text 8
W: Did you read the article about the new subway line? They're going to extend it all the way to the airport.
M: Yes, I saw that. It's about time! The traffic on the highway to the airport is terrible.
W: Exactly! And the taxi fees are so expensive. The subway will be so much more convenient.
M: When do they expect it to be finished?
W: According to the article, construction will start next spring and the line should open in about two years.
M: That's exciting news for commuters.

Text 9
M: Good afternoon. I'm here to pick up my car. I dropped it off this morning for a service.
W: Of course. Can I have your name?
M: It's Robert Taylor.
W: Let me check... Yes, Mr. Taylor. The standard oil change and filter replacement have been done.
M: Great. Was everything else okay with the car?
W: Our mechanic noticed that your front tires are getting a little worn. It's not urgent, but you might want to think about replacing them soon.
M: Thanks for the heads-up. I'll think about it. So how much do I owe?
W: That'll be one hundred and twenty dollars for today's service.

Text 10
M: Have you ever thought about volunteering abroad?
W: I've always wanted to, but I'm not sure where to start. Do you have any experience with it?
M: Actually yes. Last summer, I spent two months teaching English to children in Thailand. It was an incredible experience.
W: That sounds amazing! Was it through an organization?
M: Yes, I went through a nonprofit called Global Volunteers. They help you with all the logistics – accommodation, meals, even language training.
W: That's very helpful. I'm particularly interested in environmental work. Do they have programs for that?
M: They do. They have a marine conservation program in Costa Rica and a reforestation project in Kenya.
W: The Kenya project sounds perfect. I've always wanted to visit Africa, and I care deeply about the environment.
M: I think you'd love it. Just make sure to apply well in advance – spots fill up quickly.
questions.passage_source: 2026年1月 浙江卷 · 听力理解 (Q1-Q20)

M: That's exciting news for commuters.

Text 9
M: Good afternoon. I'm here to pick up my car. I dropped it off this morning for a service.
W: Of course. Can I have your name?
M: It's Robert Taylor.
W: Let me check... Yes, Mr. Taylor. The standard oil change and filter replacement have been done.
M: Great. Was everything else okay with the car?
W: Our mechanic noticed that your front tires are getting a little worn. It's not urgent, but you might want to think about replacing them soon.
M: Thanks for the heads-up. I'll think about it. So how much do I owe?
W: That'll be one hundred and twenty dollars for today's service.

Text 10
M: Have you ever thought about volunteering abroad?

W: That sounds amazing! Was it through an organization?

W: That's very helpful. I'm particularly interested in environmental work. Do they have programs for that?
M: They do. They have a marine conservation program in Costa Rica and a reforestation project in Kenya.
W: The Kenya project sounds perfect. I've always wanted to visit Africa, and I care deeply about the environment.
M: I think you'd love it. Just make sure to apply well in advance – spots fill up quickly.

questions.question: When does the library close on weekdays?
questions.explanation: 参见听力原文第 1 题相关部分。
questions.passage:
Text 1
M: Do you know when the library closes today?
W: Let me check. It says here it closes at nine on weekdays.
M: Great, I still have a few hours then.

Text 2
W: I'm thinking of joining a running club. Do you know of any good ones nearby?
M: Yes, there's one that meets at the park every Saturday morning. I've been a member for two years.
W: Sounds perfect! How do I sign up?
M: Just show up at the park at eight and bring your ID. They'll register you on the spot.

Text 3
M: What are you doing this weekend? Are you going to the annual company picnic?
W: I was planning to, but I just found out my sister is coming to visit. I haven't seen her in months, so I'd rather spend time with her.
M: That's understandable. Family comes first.

Text 4
W: How's your new apartment? Did you finally finish unpacking?
M: Almost! I still have a few boxes of books left. The furniture is all set up, though, and I love how spacious the living room is.
W: That's great. Are your neighbors friendly?
M: Very! The couple next door brought over a welcome cake last week. I feel really settled in now.

Text 5
W: This traffic is awful. We're going to be late for the concert.
M: Don't worry. I just got a text from Jake saying they haven't even started yet. Apparently the opening act is still setting up.
W: Oh, that's a relief. Maybe we'll make it in time after all.

Text 6
W: Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to the city museum from here?
M: Sure. Go straight down this road for about three blocks. When you reach the post office, turn right. The museum will be on your left, just next to the park.
W: Is it far? Should I take a taxi?
M: No, it's only a ten-minute walk. It's a lovely day too, so I'd recommend walking.
W: Thank you so much. That's very helpful.

Text 7
M: I really liked that documentary about ocean pollution. Did you watch it?
W: Yes, I did! I was really disturbed by the images of sea creatures trapped in plastic waste.
M: Me too. But on the bright side, the part about new technologies for cleaning up the ocean was inspiring.
W: Absolutely! That robot that collects plastic from the sea floor was incredible.
M: I hope more countries adopt these innovations. We really need to take action.

Text 8
W: Have you decided what to do for your gap year?
M: I'm thinking of travelling through South America. I want to improve my Spanish and experience different cultures.
W: That sounds wonderful. Have you thought about what countries you'll visit?
M: I definitely want to spend time in Peru and Argentina. I might also pass through Chile if time allows.
W: What about accommodation? Hotels can get expensive.
M: I'm planning to stay at hostels mainly and do some work exchanges where I help out on local farms in exchange for food and a bed.

Text 9
W: It's so disappointing. I studied for weeks for that history exam, but I only got a C.
M: Really? What went wrong?
W: I think I focused too much on memorizing dates and names, but the exam required more critical thinking and essay writing.
M: That's a common mistake. You have to understand the "why" behind historical events, not just the "what" and "when".
W: My teacher said the same thing. She suggested I join her discussion group that meets on Thursdays.
M: That sounds like a great idea. Understanding and debate are the keys to excelling in history.
W: I know. I'll start attending next week.

Text 10
M: Good morning. I'm calling about the advertisement for the sales manager position.
W: Good morning. Thank you for calling. Can I take your name, please?
M: Yes, it's Daniel Harris.
W: Thank you, Mr. Harris. Could you tell me a bit about your background?
M: Certainly. I have eight years of experience in retail sales. For the last three years, I've been managing a team of twelve salespeople at a fashion company.
W: Impressive. And what's your reason for wanting to leave your current position?
M: I'm looking for a bigger challenge and an opportunity to work in the technology sector. Your company's reputation for innovation really attracted me.
W: That's great. We'll need you to come in for an interview. Are you available this week?
M: I'm free on Wednesday and Friday afternoons.
W: Let's schedule you for Friday at two o'clock. Does that work for you?
M: That works perfectly. Thank you.
questions.passage_source: 2024年1月 浙江卷 · 听力理解 (Q1-Q20)

W: I was planning to, but I just found out my sister is coming to visit. I haven't seen her in months, so I'd rather spend time with her.
M: That's understandable. Family comes first.

Text 4
W: How's your new apartment? Did you finally finish unpacking?
M: Almost! I still have a few boxes of books left. The furniture is all set up, though, and I love how spacious the living room is.
W: That's great. Are your neighbors friendly?
M: Very! The couple next door brought over a welcome cake last week. I feel really settled in now.

Text 5
W: This traffic is awful. We're going to be late for the concert.
M: Don't worry. I just got a text from Jake saying they haven't even started yet. Apparently the opening act is still setting up.
W: Oh, that's a relief. Maybe we'll make it in time after all.

Text 6
W: Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to the city museum from here?
M: Sure. Go straight down this road for about three blocks. When you reach the post office, turn right. The museum will be on your left, just next to the park.
W: Is it far? Should I take a taxi?
M: No, it's only a ten-minute walk. It's a lovely day too, so I'd recommend walking.
W: Thank you so much. That's very helpful.

Text 7
M: I really liked that documentary about ocean pollution. Did you watch it?
W: Yes, I did! I was really disturbed by the images of sea creatures trapped in plastic waste.
M: Me too. But on the bright side, the part about new technologies for cleaning up the ocean was inspiring.
W: Absolutely! That robot that collects plastic from the sea floor was incredible.
M: I hope more countries adopt these innovations. We really need to take action.

Text 8
W: Have you decided what to do for your gap year?
M: I'm thinking of travelling through South America. I want to improve my Spanish and experience different cultures.
W: That sounds wonderful. Have you thought about what countries you'll visit?
M: I definitely want to spend time in Peru and Argentina. I might also pass through Chile if time allows.
W: What about accommodation? Hotels can get expensive.
M: I'm planning to stay at hostels mainly and do some work exchanges where I help out on local farms in exchange for food and a bed.

Text 9
W: It's so disappointing. I studied for weeks for that history exam, but I only got a C.
M: Really? What went wrong?
W: I think I focused too much on memorizing dates and names, but the exam required more critical thinking and essay writing.
M: That's a common mistake. You have to understand the "why" behind historical events, not just the "what" and "when".
W: My teacher said the same thing. She suggested I join her discussion group that meets on Thursdays.
M: That sounds like a great idea. Understanding and debate are the keys to excelling in history.
W: I know. I'll start attending next week.

Text 10
M: Good morning. I'm calling about the advertisement for the sales manager position.
W: Good morning. Thank you for calling. Can I take your name, please?
M: Yes, it's Daniel Harris.
W: Thank you, Mr. Harris. Could you tell me a bit about your background?
M: Certainly. I have eight years of experience in retail sales. For the last three years, I've been managing a team of twelve salespeople at a fashion company.
W: Impressive. And what's your reason for wanting to leave your current position?
M: I'm looking for a bigger challenge and an opportunity to work in the technology sector. Your company's reputation for innovation really attracted me.
W: That's great. We'll need you to come in for an interview. Are you available this week?
M: I'm free on Wednesday and Friday afternoons.
W: Let's schedule you for Friday at two o'clock. Does that work for you?
M: That works perfectly. Thank you.
questions.passage_source: 2024年1月 浙江卷 · 听力理解 (Q1-Q20)

M: Sure. Go straight down this road for about three blocks. When you reach the post office, turn right. The museum will be on your left, just next to the park.
W: Is it far? Should I take a taxi?
M: No, it's only a ten-minute walk. It's a lovely day too, so I'd recommend walking.
W: Thank you so much. That's very helpful.

Text 7
M: I really liked that documentary about ocean pollution. Did you watch it?
W: Yes, I did! I was really disturbed by the images of sea creatures trapped in plastic waste.
M: Me too. But on the bright side, the part about new technologies for cleaning up the ocean was inspiring.
W: Absolutely! That robot that collects plastic from the sea floor was incredible.
M: I hope more countries adopt these innovations. We really need to take action.

Text 8
W: Have you decided what to do for your gap year?
M: I'm thinking of travelling through South America. I want to improve my Spanish and experience different cultures.
W: That sounds wonderful. Have you thought about what countries you'll visit?
M: I definitely want to spend time in Peru and Argentina. I might also pass through Chile if time allows.
W: What about accommodation? Hotels can get expensive.
M: I'm planning to stay at hostels mainly and do some work exchanges where I help out on local farms in exchange for food and a bed.

Text 9
W: It's so disappointing. I studied for weeks for that history exam, but I only got a C.
M: Really? What went wrong?
W: I think I focused too much on memorizing dates and names, but the exam required more critical thinking and essay writing.
M: That's a common mistake. You have to understand the "why" behind historical events, not just the "what" and "when".
W: My teacher said the same thing. She suggested I join her discussion group that meets on Thursdays.
M: That sounds like a great idea. Understanding and debate are the keys to excelling in history.
W: I know. I'll start attending next week.

Text 10
M: Good morning. I'm calling about the advertisement for the sales manager position.
W: Good morning. Thank you for calling. Can I take your name, please?
M: Yes, it's Daniel Harris.
W: Thank you, Mr. Harris. Could you tell me a bit about your background?
M: Certainly. I have eight years of experience in retail sales. For the last three years, I've been managing a team of twelve salespeople at a fashion company.
W: Impressive. And what's your reason for wanting to leave your current position?
M: I'm looking for a bigger challenge and an opportunity to work in the technology sector. Your company's reputation for innovation really attracted me.
W: That's great. We'll need you to come in for an interview. Are you available this week?
M: I'm free on Wednesday and Friday afternoons.
W: Let's schedule you for Friday at two o'clock. Does that work for you?
M: That works perfectly. Thank you.
questions.passage_source: 2024年1月 浙江卷 · 听力理解 (Q1-Q20)

questions.question: Why hasn't the woman received her order?

options.A: The restaurant ran out of chicken

questions.explanation: 参见听力原文第 1 题相关部分。
questions.passage:
Text 1
W: Excuse me, I ordered a chicken sandwich about twenty minutes ago. I haven't received it yet.
M: I apologize for the delay. The kitchen is a bit backed up. Your order should be out within the next five minutes.

Text 2
M: Do you know if the post office is open on Sundays?
W: I'm not sure. I think it opens at noon and closes at five.
M: That's helpful. Thanks.

Text 3
M: Mom, I'm thinking of trying out for the school play. Do you think I should?
W: Absolutely! You were great in the drama class last year. This could be an excellent opportunity for you to develop your confidence.
M: You really think so? I'm a little nervous.
W: I know you are, but try not to focus on the nerves. Think about how much fun it'll be to perform on stage.

Text 4
W: We just got the project deadline moved up by two weeks.
M: That's stressful. Do you think we can make it?
W: We'll have to work overtime and maybe bring in an extra team member. But yes, I think we can do it.

Text 5
M: The forecast says it might snow this weekend. Are you still planning to drive up to the cabin?
W: Yes, but I'll check the road conditions first. If the roads are icy, I'll take the train instead.

Text 6
M: Hi, welcome back! I haven't seen you in a while.
W: Yes, it's been a few months. I moved to a new neighbourhood and found a gym closer to home, but I missed the classes here, so I'm back.
M: That's great to hear! Have there been any changes in your fitness goals since we last spoke?
W: Actually, yes. I've been dealing with some lower back pain, so I need to focus on strengthening exercises rather than cardio for now.
M: Got it. We have a new instructor who specializes in rehabilitation exercises. I'll make sure to introduce you.
W: That would be wonderful, thank you.

Text 7
W: I just read that the city is launching a new bike-share program next month.
M: I heard about that too. They're placing stations every half mile throughout the city.
W: And the pricing is really reasonable – just one dollar an hour or five dollars for a full day.
M: Great for tourists and commuters alike. Do you think you'll use it?
W: Definitely for short trips. But for longer distances, I'd still prefer my own bike.
M: Same here. But it's a great initiative for reducing traffic congestion.

Text 8
W: I can't believe the game last night. Did you watch it?
M: Of course! I was on the edge of my seat in the final quarter.
W: Me too. I thought our team was definitely going to lose when they were ten points behind with three minutes left.
M: But then Johnson made that incredible three-pointer and the crowd went wild.
W: And then Miller's game-winning shot with just two seconds left – I nearly screamed!
M: It was definitely one of the best games this season. I hope they keep up this performance in the play-offs.

Text 9
M: I heard you were in an accident last week. Are you alright?
W: Yes, I'm fine now, thanks. A car ran a red light and hit the side of my vehicle.
M: That must have been frightening. Was anyone else hurt?
W: No, thankfully. The other driver got out and immediately checked on me. He was very apologetic.
M: What happened with the police?
W: They came quickly and filed a report. My car had some damage, but nothing too serious. Insurance should cover most of it.
M: That's a relief. Have you recovered from the shock?
W: Mostly yes. I'm still a little nervous about driving, but my husband has been very supportive.

Text 10
W: Hello, I'm calling to enquire about the summer photography workshop you're offering.
M: Hello! Yes, we have a wonderful program this year. It runs for two weeks in July.
W: What level is it aimed at? I've been taking photos for about two years, but I'm self-taught.
M: That's perfect. It's aimed at intermediate learners – people who know the basics but want to take their skills to the next level.
W: Great. What does the program cover?
M: The first week focuses on technical skills like lighting, composition, and editing software. The second week is more creative – we take field trips to interesting locations and you'll work on a personal project.
W: That sounds wonderful. How many students are there in each session?
M: We keep the groups small – just twelve students per session – so you get a lot of personal attention from the instructor.
W: And how much does it cost?
M: The full two-week course is eight hundred dollars, which includes all materials and a printed portfolio at the end.
W: That seems reasonable. I'd like to sign up. What's the next step?
M: I'll send you an email with the registration form. Can I have your email address?
questions.passage_source: 2023年1月 浙江卷 · 听力理解 (Q1-Q20)

M: That's a relief. Have you recovered from the shock?
W: Mostly yes. I'm still a little nervous about driving, but my husband has been very supportive.

Text 10
W: Hello, I'm calling to enquire about the summer photography workshop you're offering.
M: Hello! Yes, we have a wonderful program this year. It runs for two weeks in July.
W: What level is it aimed at? I've been taking photos for about two years, but I'm self-taught.
M: That's perfect. It's aimed at intermediate learners – people who know the basics but want to take their skills to the next level.
W: Great. What does the program cover?
M: The first week focuses on technical skills like lighting, composition, and editing software. The second week is more creative – we take field trips to interesting locations and you'll work on a personal project.
W: That sounds wonderful. How many students are there in each session?
M: We keep the groups small – just twelve students per session – so you get a lot of personal attention from the instructor.
W: And how much does it cost?
M: The full two-week course is eight hundred dollars, which includes all materials and a printed portfolio at the end.
W: That seems reasonable. I'd like to sign up. What's the next step?
M: I'll send you an email with the registration form. Can I have your email address?

questions.passage:
Text 1
W: Excuse me, I ordered a chicken sandwich about twenty minutes ago. I haven't received it yet.
M: I apologize for the delay. The kitchen is a bit backed up. Your order should be out within the next five minutes.

Text 2
M: Do you know if the post office is open on Sundays?
W: I'm not sure. I think it opens at noon and closes at five.
M: That's helpful. Thanks.

Text 3
M: Mom, I'm thinking of trying out for the school play. Do you think I should?
W: Absolutely! You were great in the drama class last year. This could be an excellent opportunity for you to develop your confidence.
M: You really think so? I'm a little nervous.
W: I know you are, but try not to focus on the nerves. Think about how much fun it'll be to perform on stage.

Text 4
W: We just got the project deadline moved up by two weeks.

Text 7

W: And how much does it cost?
M: The full two-week course is eight hundred dollars, which includes all materials and a printed portfolio at the end.
W: That seems reasonable. I'd like to sign up. What's the next step?
M: I'll send you an email with the registration form. Can I have your email address?

questions.explanation: 参见听力原文第 11 题相关部分。
questions.passage:
Text 1
W: Excuse me, I ordered a chicken sandwich about twenty minutes ago. I haven't received it yet.
M: I apologize for the delay. The kitchen is a bit backed up. Your order should be out within the next five minutes.

Text 2
M: Do you know if the post office is open on Sundays?
W: I'm not sure. I think it opens at noon and closes at five.
M: That's helpful. Thanks.

Text 3
M: Mom, I'm thinking of trying out for the school play. Do you think I should?
W: Absolutely! You were great in the drama class last year. This could be an excellent opportunity for you to develop your confidence.
M: You really think so? I'm a little nervous.
W: I know you are, but try not to focus on the nerves. Think about how much fun it'll be to perform on stage.

Text 4
W: We just got the project deadline moved up by two weeks.
M: That's stressful. Do you think we can make it?
W: We'll have to work overtime and maybe bring in an extra team member. But yes, I think we can do it.

Text 5
M: The forecast says it might snow this weekend. Are you still planning to drive up to the cabin?
W: Yes, but I'll check the road conditions first. If the roads are icy, I'll take the train instead.

Text 6
M: Hi, welcome back! I haven't seen you in a while.
W: Yes, it's been a few months. I moved to a new neighbourhood and found a gym closer to home, but I missed the classes here, so I'm back.
M: That's great to hear! Have there been any changes in your fitness goals since we last spoke?
W: Actually, yes. I've been dealing with some lower back pain, so I need to focus on strengthening exercises rather than cardio for now.
M: Got it. We have a new instructor who specializes in rehabilitation exercises. I'll make sure to introduce you.
W: That would be wonderful, thank you.

Text 7
W: I just read that the city is launching a new bike-share program next month.
M: I heard about that too. They're placing stations every half mile throughout the city.
W: And the pricing is really reasonable – just one dollar an hour or five dollars for a full day.
M: Great for tourists and commuters alike. Do you think you'll use it?
W: Definitely for short trips. But for longer distances, I'd still prefer my own bike.
M: Same here. But it's a great initiative for reducing traffic congestion.

Text 8
W: I can't believe the game last night. Did you watch it?
M: Of course! I was on the edge of my seat in the final quarter.
W: Me too. I thought our team was definitely going to lose when they were ten points behind with three minutes left.
M: But then Johnson made that incredible three-pointer and the crowd went wild.
W: And then Miller's game-winning shot with just two seconds left – I nearly screamed!
M: It was definitely one of the best games this season. I hope they keep up this performance in the play-offs.

Text 9
M: I heard you were in an accident last week. Are you alright?
W: Yes, I'm fine now, thanks. A car ran a red light and hit the side of my vehicle.
M: That must have been frightening. Was anyone else hurt?
W: No, thankfully. The other driver got out and immediately checked on me. He was very apologetic.
M: What happened with the police?
W: They came quickly and filed a report. My car had some damage, but nothing too serious. Insurance should cover most of it.
M: That's a relief. Have you recovered from the shock?
W: Mostly yes. I'm still a little nervous about driving, but my husband has been very supportive.

Text 10
W: Hello, I'm calling to enquire about the summer photography workshop you're offering.
M: Hello! Yes, we have a wonderful program this year. It runs for two weeks in July.
W: What level is it aimed at? I've been taking photos for about two years, but I'm self-taught.
M: That's perfect. It's aimed at intermediate learners – people who know the basics but want to take their skills to the next level.
W: Great. What does the program cover?
M: The first week focuses on technical skills like lighting, composition, and editing software. The second week is more creative – we take field trips to interesting locations and you'll work on a personal project.
W: That sounds wonderful. How many students are there in each session?
M: We keep the groups small – just twelve students per session – so you get a lot of personal attention from the instructor.
W: And how much does it cost?
M: The full two-week course is eight hundred dollars, which includes all materials and a printed portfolio at the end.
W: That seems reasonable. I'd like to sign up. What's the next step?
M: I'll send you an email with the registration form. Can I have your email address?
questions.passage_source: 2023年1月 浙江卷 · 听力理解 (Q1-Q20)

questions.question: What happened to the woman last week?

options.A: She was involved in a car accident

W: Absolutely! You were great in the drama class last year. This could be an excellent opportunity for you to develop your confidence.
M: You really think so? I'm a little nervous.
W: I know you are, but try not to focus on the nerves. Think about how much fun it'll be to perform on stage.

Text 4
W: We just got the project deadline moved up by two weeks.
M: That's stressful. Do you think we can make it?
W: We'll have to work overtime and maybe bring in an extra team member. But yes, I think we can do it.

Text 5
M: The forecast says it might snow this weekend. Are you still planning to drive up to the cabin?
W: Yes, but I'll check the road conditions first. If the roads are icy, I'll take the train instead.

Text 6
M: Hi, welcome back! I haven't seen you in a while.
W: Yes, it's been a few months. I moved to a new neighbourhood and found a gym closer to home, but I missed the classes here, so I'm back.
M: That's great to hear! Have there been any changes in your fitness goals since we last spoke?
W: Actually, yes. I've been dealing with some lower back pain, so I need to focus on strengthening exercises rather than cardio for now.
M: Got it. We have a new instructor who specializes in rehabilitation exercises. I'll make sure to introduce you.
W: That would be wonderful, thank you.

Text 7
W: I just read that the city is launching a new bike-share program next month.
M: I heard about that too. They're placing stations every half mile throughout the city.
W: And the pricing is really reasonable – just one dollar an hour or five dollars for a full day.
M: Great for tourists and commuters alike. Do you think you'll use it?
W: Definitely for short trips. But for longer distances, I'd still prefer my own bike.
M: Same here. But it's a great initiative for reducing traffic congestion.

Text 8
W: I can't believe the game last night. Did you watch it?
M: Of course! I was on the edge of my seat in the final quarter.
W: Me too. I thought our team was definitely going to lose when they were ten points behind with three minutes left.
M: But then Johnson made that incredible three-pointer and the crowd went wild.
W: And then Miller's game-winning shot with just two seconds left – I nearly screamed!
M: It was definitely one of the best games this season. I hope they keep up this performance in the play-offs.

Text 9
M: I heard you were in an accident last week. Are you alright?
W: Yes, I'm fine now, thanks. A car ran a red light and hit the side of my vehicle.
M: That must have been frightening. Was anyone else hurt?
W: No, thankfully. The other driver got out and immediately checked on me. He was very apologetic.
M: What happened with the police?
W: They came quickly and filed a report. My car had some damage, but nothing too serious. Insurance should cover most of it.
M: That's a relief. Have you recovered from the shock?
W: Mostly yes. I'm still a little nervous about driving, but my husband has been very supportive.

Text 10
W: Hello, I'm calling to enquire about the summer photography workshop you're offering.
M: Hello! Yes, we have a wonderful program this year. It runs for two weeks in July.
W: What level is it aimed at? I've been taking photos for about two years, but I'm self-taught.
M: That's perfect. It's aimed at intermediate learners – people who know the basics but want to take their skills to the next level.
W: Great. What does the program cover?
M: The first week focuses on technical skills like lighting, composition, and editing software. The second week is more creative – we take field trips to interesting locations and you'll work on a personal project.
W: That sounds wonderful. How many students are there in each session?
M: We keep the groups small – just twelve students per session – so you get a lot of personal attention from the instructor.
W: And how much does it cost?
M: The full two-week course is eight hundred dollars, which includes all materials and a printed portfolio at the end.
W: That seems reasonable. I'd like to sign up. What's the next step?
M: I'll send you an email with the registration form. Can I have your email address?
questions.passage_source: 2023年1月 浙江卷 · 听力理解 (Q1-Q20)

questions.question: How much does the full two-week course cost?
